title: ADS I19 shop families, I76/I77 camera recovery, I28 solo notice, and I4 setup wizards
objective: Implement and verify deterministic NPC shop-family support, ADS-owned duty camera recovery, once-per-entry solo-duty leave guidance, and five independent replayable setup wizards as one unversioned change set.
minimum_success: All four ticket areas are implemented with preserved public shop contracts, safe callback-free failure behavior, configuration migration, operator UI and documentation, and full C# and Python suites passing.
exceptional_success: Targeted regression coverage proves every requested family, gate, cooldown, latch, migration, and replay behavior; generated catalog/audit are deterministic and the complete diff is warning-free and ready for user-triggered live acceptance.
boundaries: Include I19, I76/I77, I28, and I4 only.
Do not bump assembly version, package, publish, commit, push, or perform live-game purchases/actions.
Keep public shop commands, IPC names, capability schema, phase strings, and failure-code strings compatible.
existing_assets: Existing duty context, ownership, UI window system, and test seams
Pinned local placement inputs and seven Python generator tests
User-supplied implementation plan and acceptance matrix
constraints: Fail closed before callbacks when live UI identity, row, costs, rank, capacity, confirmation, or balance cannot be proved.
Use only Dalamud/game input APIs; never OS-level input.
Preserve unrelated current behavior and existing compatibility no-ops.
current_step: Reconcile repository state, required backups, and owning seams before editing.
next_action: Save the pre-edit baseline/change manifest and create required backups, then implement camera and solo notice services.
